articles.title: STILL NEED TO PROVE IT
articles.summary: Sprout Social's new research [5] surveyed 1,200+ marketing directors and CMOs. Turns out, confidence in social media is high... but trust in ROI models is low. For SMMs, that means more pressure to tie strategy to outcomes.

articles.title: 20% OF AMERICANS USE AI TOOLS 10X+/MONTH, BUT GROWTH IS SLOWING AND TRADITIONAL SEARCH HASN'T DIPPED
articles.summary: AI tool adoption in the US is slowing, while traditional search remains dominant. Over 20% of Americans now use AI tools 10 or more times per month, up from 3% in early 2023. Despite this, 95% of Americans continue to use traditional search engines, with 86% as heavy users, indicating that AI complements rather than replaces search.

articles.title: PIXABILITY: CPG FOOD & BEVERAGE CATEGORY TRENDS ON YOUTUBE
articles.summary: The Food & Beverage category on YouTube is the platform's fourth-largest, with 5.9 trillion views and 6.7 million channels. It's driven 99% by creators. Short-form and humorous content dominates, with top creators and fast-growing channels seeing view increases up to 14,000% YoY. Successful brands combine paid and organic support. They also partner with creators and adopt Shorts and quick-cut styles.

articles.title: TIKTOK SHOP STRATEGY: SOMERCE'S CREATOR COMMUNITY METHOD
articles.summary: Somerce manages creator communities as long-term partners rather than one-off promotional channels. For each client, Somerce runs a dedicated creator network, gifting samples and incentivizing participation through cash rewards, product perks, and competitions. This model has driven strong results in beauty, such as Glow For It's 1,200-creator community with 800 actively posting. Operating on low retainers with performance-based commissions, Somerce combines paid top influencers for reach with smaller creators for scalable content and sales.

articles.title: WIN REPORT: WHEN USER BEHAVIOR SURPRISES YOU, PAY ATTENTION
articles.summary: A heatmap of a high-traffic product page showed that many users clicked on the Product Specification section, even though it was hidden in an accordion. The client's original page kept this section closed by default, but a test variation opened it automatically. This small change led to a 12% increase in transactions. The result shows how analyzing user behavior and making simple adjustments can produce significant gains.

articles.title: TIP: POST-MEETING VIDEO SUMMARIES
articles.summary: Instead of sending routine post-meeting emails that get ignored, turn meeting notes into a 30-sec AI-generated video. Include slides that summarize challenges, proposed solutions, and next steps. Add the company logo, reference stakeholders by name, and include a meaningful data point. Video tools like Synthesia or Loom AI let you track who watches and how much, so you can tailor follow-ups based on engagement.

articles.title: MKT1'S BEST FRAMEWORKS FOR PRODUCT MARKETING, CONTENT MARKETING, AND GROWTH MARKETING
articles.summary: This guide outlines MKT1's core frameworks for building effective product, content, and growth marketing strategies. It defines product marketing as audience-first research that informs positioning, audience analysis, and ecosystem mapping, which are critical to standing out against competitors and shaping a company's market narrative. It recommends defining TAM and ICPs, prioritizing them into tiers, and building an Ecosystem Map that considers competitors, complements, and influencers rather than only direct rivals. Content should be treated like a product, guided by frameworks like Story Stack and Perceptions, with a focus on quality, strategic roadmapping, and repurposing to maximize Mileage. For growth, select from six core engines, adopt Account-Driven GTM, mapping both account and contact lifecycle stages, and use bottom-up forecasting with efficiency metrics.

articles.title: REDDIT PRO GOES MOBILE: TOOLS FOR THE AI DISCOVERY ERA
articles.summary: Reddit has launched its most significant Reddit Pro update, available now on iOS and coming later this year on Android. The update adds mobile access to the Trends tool, allowing businesses to track mentions, keywords, and engagement opportunities in real time. It also introduces enhanced performance insights for comments to help measure which responses build trust and drive meaningful engagement. In addition, Reddit released its first Reddit Pro Organic Playbook, a guide to help businesses understand Reddit's culture and apply proven strategies for authentic community participation.

articles.title: FROM OVERWHELMED TO CONFIDENT: YOUR 90-DAY PLAN TO THRIVE AS A NEW PMM MANAGER
articles.summary: New PMM managers often face overwhelm as AI accelerates productivity but also creates more noise, lower motivation, and unrealistic executive expectations. Success in the role depends on mastering four foundations: purpose, people, process, and performance. Process is becoming increasingly important in an AI-driven environment. This post introduces a 30-60-90 day plan that helps managers transition from consuming value to producing it by first building trust and clarity, then refining systems, and finally delivering visible wins tied to business impact. By day 90, managers should establish a PMM charter, delegate ownership, and cement a high-performing culture while also demonstrating their own leadership through impactful projects. Long-term success comes from giving clear feedback, creating scalable systems, and shifting from individual contribution to enabling team-wide growth.

articles.title: GOOGLE: WE'RE TESTING CHANGES TO AI MODE TO ENCOURAGE CLICKS
articles.summary: Google is testing changes to AI Mode in Search to drive more clicks to publishers. Updates include adding embedded link carousels to both desktop and mobile, improving inline links for better context, and expanding the Web Guide feature from the “Web” tab to the main “All” tab.

articles.title: SUBSTACK JUST KILLED THE CREATOR ECONOMY
articles.summary: Substack now requires writers to use Apple's in-app purchases, raising prices 30% on iOS, delaying payouts 45 days, and removing the option to opt out. Apple controls billing and subscriber data, blocking writers from taking their audience elsewhere. This shift from writer-owned Stripe accounts to Substack lock-in reflects a broader trend of platform control, dependency, and fee hikes across the creator economy.

articles.title: STUCK IN A RUT? 8 IDEATION STRATEGIES TO SPARK YOUR NEXT VIDEO
articles.summary: Reinvent past videos, poll audiences, and use the “opposite day” approach.

articles.title: CRACKER BARREL SCRAPS NEW LOGO AFTER BACKLASH
